item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents oppose society taking general priority over individual rights throughout. Agent A consistently argues that public-order or common-interest language can be abused, while allowing narrow accountable emergency limits. Agent B maintains the same rights-first view and ends with stronger wording, but this is intensification rather than a flip. Both engage the same themes directly, though much of the exchange is repetitive because their positions are already aligned.
